sanskrit: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु । मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे ॥
hindiTranslation: तू मेरा भक्त हो जा, मेरेमें मनवाला हो जा, मेरा पूजन करनेवाला हो जा और मेरेको नमस्कार कर ।  ऐसा करनेसे तू मेरेको ही प्राप्त हो जायगा  यह मैं तेरे सामने सत्य प्रतिज्ञा करता हूँ क्योंकि तू मेरा अत्यन्त प्रिय है ॥
englishTranslation: Fix your mind on me, become my devotee, worship me, and bow to me. You will come to me; I promise you this truly, because you are dear to me.
essence: Wholehearted devotion becomes the shortest path home.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna asks Arjuna to keep his mind on him, love him, worship him, and show reverence. He promises that this wholehearted devotion leads to union with him.
simpleInsight: Devotion is not vague feeling; it becomes a whole-life orientation that steadies the heart.
detailedMeaning: On the edge of battle, Arjuna is still wavering. Krishna now gives the simplest direct path: keep your mind on me, love me, worship me, and bow in reverence. This is the final turn from hesitation to complete surrender.
modernRelevance: You are standing outside a hospital room, about to say the one honest sentence that could change everything. This verse asks for a single-hearted turn toward what is highest, even when fear is loud.
translationLiteral: मन्मना (with mind on me) / भव (become) / मद्भक्तः (my devotee) / मद्याजी (my worshipper) / माम् (to me) / नमस्कुरु (bow) / माम् (me) / एव (only) / एष्यसि (you will come) / सत्यम् (truth) / ते (to you) / प्रतिजाने (I promise) / प्रियः (dear) / असि (you are) / मे (to me)
insight: It shifts devotion from sentiment to commitment: attention, love, action, and humility all point in one direction.

quoteVariants.text: Fix your mind on me. Love me, bow to me, and you will come to me.
Mind on me. Love. Bow. Come home.
You are dear to me, and I will not let you go.
Turn to me with your whole heart.
